---
title: "Pathway_analysis_RNAseq"
author: "Johannes Schöneich"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r libraries}
library(Hmisc)
library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)
library(digest)
library(cowplot)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(stats)
library(broom)
library(multcomp)
library(rstatix)
library(circlize)
library(openxlsx)
library(glue)
library(reshape2)
library(progeny)
library(decoupleR)
library(OmnipathR)
library(GSVA)
library(msigdbr)

#Specify the harddrive
harddrive<- "H:/231248/NTFS_3.64TB"

# we defined the functions in the R-script pathway_functions
source(file.path(harddrive,"helper_functions.R"))
source(file.path(harddrive,"DE_analysis/Analysis_scripts/Pathway/pathway_functions.R"))

set.seed(1234)
```

```{r }
# condition which has been analysed e.g. timepoint, infection, crypt-villus
condition <- "Proteomics"

analysis_folder <- "DE_analysis/DE_Proteomics"

cm.norm <- read.csv(file.path(harddrive,"Proteomics/raw_data_protdf.csv"),row.names = 1)

plots_folder <- file.path(harddrive,analysis_folder,"Pathway/plots")
charts_folder <- file.path(harddrive,analysis_folder,"Pathway/charts")

dir.create(plots_folder,recursive = T)
dir.create(charts_folder,recursive = T)

#Read in the results.list
#results.list <- readRDS(file.path(harddrive,analysis_folder,glue("DE_results.list_{condition}.Rds")))

limma_results <- read.csv(file.path(harddrive,
                          "DE_analysis/DE_Proteomics/charts/limma_results_timepoints.csv"),
                                    row.names = 1)

limma_results$comparison <- as.factor(limma_results$comparison)

results.list <- split(limma_results,limma_results$comparison)

names(results.list) <- c("d01_d05","d01_d10","d01_d25","d05_d10","d05_d25","d10_d25")

de.list <- lapply(results.list, function(x){as.data.frame(x)})
```
# progeny + decoupleR
```{r}
net <- get_progeny(organism = 'mouse', top = 100)

sample_acts <- run_wmean(mat = cm.norm, net = net, .source = 'source', .target = 'target',
                         .mor = 'weight', times = 100, minsize = 5)

sample_acts_mat <- sample_acts %>%
  filter(statistic == 'norm_wmean') %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = 'condition', names_from = 'source',
              values_from = 'score') %>%
  column_to_rownames('condition') %>%
  as.matrix()

# Scale per sample
sample_acts_mat <- scale(sample_acts_mat)
sample_acts_mat <- sample_acts_mat[,sort(colnames(sample_acts_mat))]

write.xlsx(sample_acts_mat, file = file.path(charts_folder, 
                                             "sample_acts_scaled.xlsx"), 
           rowNames = TRUE)

# Choose color palette
palette_length <- 100
my_color <- colorRampPalette(c("Darkblue", "white","red"))(palette_length)

my_breaks <- c(seq(-3, 0, length.out=ceiling(palette_length/2) + 1),
               seq(0.05, 3, length.out=floor(palette_length/2)))

# Plot
progeny_heatmap <- pheatmap(t(sample_acts_mat), border_color = NA, 
                          color = my_color, breaks = my_breaks,cluster_rows = F,
                          cluster_cols = F)
```

```{r save progeny heatmap}
png(file.path(plots_folder,"png",glue("progeny_heatmap_{condition}.png")),
    width=40,height=20,units="cm",res = 300)
draw(progeny_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()

pdf(file.path(plots_folder,"pdf",glue("progeny_heatmap_{condition}.pdf")),
    width=40,height=20)
draw(progeny_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()
```

# plot progeny as boxplot
```{r}
# Reshape the data into a long format
m_df <- melt(t(sample_acts_mat))

colnames(m_df)<-c("pathway","timepoint","value")

# Extract the timepoint from the variable name
m_df$timepoint <- str_remove(m_df$timepoint,"_+[1-9]")

if(0){
m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %in% c("C01","C05","C10")]="pre-wea_crypts"
m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %in% c("V01","V05","V10")]="pre-wea_villi"
m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %in% "C25"]="post-wea_crypts"
m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %in% "V25"]="post-wea_villi"

m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %ni% "d25"]="prWe"
m_df$timepoint[m_df$timepoint %in% "d25"]="poWe"
}
#m_df$timepoint <- factor(m_df$timepoint,levels = levels(m_df$timepoint)[c(2,1)],ordered = TRUE)

m_df$timepoint <- as.factor(m_df$timepoint)
#m_df$timepoint <- factor(m_df$timepoint,levels = levels(m_df$timepoint)[c(2,1)],ordered = TRUE)

# 
# #Filter only the fitting pathways
# m_df <- m_df %>% filter(pathway %in% c("JAK-STAT","MAPK","PI3K","TGFb",
#                                        "WNT","Hypoxia","NFkB","TNFa"))
# 
# m_df$pathway <- droplevels(m_df$pathway)
# m_df$pathway <- factor(m_df$pathway, levels = c("JAK-STAT","MAPK","PI3K","TGFb",
#                                        "WNT","Hypoxia","NFkB","TNFa"))

# Perform ANOVA + Tukey test for each pathway
pathways <- unique(m_df$pathway)
results <- list()

for (p in pathways) {
  data <- m_df %>% filter(pathway == p) %>% dplyr::select(-pathway)
  result <- aov(value ~ timepoint, data = data)
  results[[p]] <- result
  # Extract ANOVA results for each pathway
  print(paste("Pathway:", p))
  print(summary(result))
}


#post-hoc tukey test
stat.test <- m_df %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ timepoint,p.adjust.method = "BH")

stat.test <- stat.test %>% add_y_position()

stat.test[!((stat.test$group1 == "d01" & stat.test$group2== "d05") |
            (stat.test$group1 == "d05" & stat.test$group2 == "d10") |
            (stat.test$group1 == "d10" & stat.test$group2 == "d25")),
          "p.adj.signif"] <- "ns"

# Create the boxplot with p-values
progeny_boxplot <- ggboxplot(m_df, 
                             x = "timepoint", 
                             y = "value",
                             fill = "timepoint",
                             facet.by = "pathway",
                             palette = uninf_cols,
                             ncol = 2,
                             size = 0.1,
                             outlier.size = 0.1) +
  ggtitle(element_blank()) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,# hjust = 1, 
                                   size = 5
                                   ),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(), 
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(), #transparent panel bg
        plot.background = element_blank(), #transparent plot bg
        aspect.ratio = 1        ) + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(stat.test, 
                     label = "p.adj.signif",
                     tip.length = 0.01,
                     hide.ns = T) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1)))
```


```{r}
save_ggplot_formats(
  plt = progeny_boxplot,
  create_plot_subdir = TRUE,
  base_plot_dir = plots_folder,
  plt_name = glue("PROGENy_boxplot_{condition}_v5"),
  width = 4,height = 8
)
```

# 2. Load Notch gene set

```{r}
# Get Notch-related genes from Reactome for mouse
msigdb <- msigdbr(db_species = "MM", species = "Mus musculus", collection = "M2")
notch_genes <- msigdb %>% filter(grepl("NOTCH", gs_name)) %>% pull(gene_symbol)

#notch_db <- msigdb %>% filter(grepl("NOTCH", gs_name)) 
notch_genes <- unique(notch_genes)
```

# 3. Compute GSVA / ssGSEA scores
```{r}
# GSVA (Gene Set Variation Analysis) / ssGSEA computes a pathway activity score per sample
# based on expression of the genes in the gene set (here: Notch genes). 
# Unlike PROGENy, ssGSEA treats all genes equally without weighted network information.
# The ssGSEA score represents the relative activity of the Notch pathway in each sample.
# Higher scores indicate higher coordinated expression of Notch pathway genes.
# ssGSEA treats all genes equally, capturing general pathway enrichment, while PROGENy uses weighted networks.
# 
# Therefore:
# - ssGSEA scores detect trends in pathway activity across timepoints or conditions.
# - Differences reflect changes in overall expression of pathway genes.
# - Comparison with PROGENy highlights pathways with regulatory impact versus purely expression-based activity.


# Create named list of gene sets
gene_sets <- list(Notch = notch_genes)

# Build ssGSEA parameter object
gsvapar <- ssgseaParam(as.matrix(cm.norm), gene_sets)

# Compute ssGSEA scores
gsva_scores <- gsva(gsvapar)

# gsva_scores: matrix of enrichment scores (pathways x samples)
# Optionally scale per sample for visualization
gsva_scores_scaled <- t(scale(t(gsva_scores)))

write.xlsx(gsva_scores_scaled,file.path(charts_folder,
                                        "notch_scores_scaled_proteomics.xlsx"),
           rowNames = TRUE)
```

```{r}
palette_length <- 100
my_color <- colorRampPalette(c("darkblue","white","red"))(palette_length)
my_breaks <- c(seq(-3,0,length.out = ceiling(palette_length/2)+1),
               seq(0.05,3,length.out = floor(palette_length/2)))

pheatmap(gsva_scores_scaled, 
         color = my_color, 
         breaks = my_breaks,
         border_color = NA,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         main = "Notch Pathway ssGSEA Scores")

```

```{r}
# Reshape to long format for ggplot
m_df <- melt(gsva_scores_scaled)
colnames(m_df) <- c("pathway","sample","value")

# Extract timepoint from sample names
m_df$timepoint <- gsub("_+[0-9]+","", m_df$sample)
m_df$timepoint <- as.factor(m_df$timepoint)

# Perform ANOVA + Tukey HSD test
stat.test <- m_df %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ timepoint, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_y_position()

# Define colors for timepoints
uninf_cols <- c("#E08B00","#5BB300","#7997FF","#FF61C9")

# Boxplot with significance
ggboxplot(m_df, x = "timepoint", y = "value",
          fill = "timepoint", palette = uninf_cols,
          facet.by = "pathway", nrow = 1, size = 0.1, outlier.size = 0.1) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(stat.test, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = TRUE) +
  ylab("Notch Pathway ssGSEA Score")

```

```{r}
# ===== 7. ANOVA + post-hoc Tukey HSD =====
library(rstatix)

# Perform ANOVA and Tukey HSD for each pathway
stat.test <- m_df %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  anova_test(value ~ timepoint) %>%       # One-way ANOVA per pathway
  add_significance()                      # Adds p-value significance labels

# Tukey post-hoc
tukey_results <- m_df %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ timepoint, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_y_position()

# Optional: filter to relevant comparisons (e.g., consecutive timepoints)
tukey_results_filtered <- tukey_results %>%
  filter((group1 == "d01" & group2 == "d05") |
         (group1 == "d05" & group2 == "d10") |
         (group1 == "d10" & group2 == "d25"))

# Overlay Tukey significance on boxplot
uninf_cols <- c("#E08B00","#5BB300","#7997FF","#FF61C9")

pathway_boxplot <- ggboxplot(m_df, x = "timepoint", y = "value",
          fill = "timepoint", palette = uninf_cols,
          facet.by = "pathway", nrow = 1, size = 0.1, outlier.size = 0.1) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tukey_results_filtered, 
                     label = "p.adj.signif", 
                     tip.length = 0.01, 
                     hide.ns = TRUE) +
  ylab("Notch Pathway ssGSEA Score")+
    theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(), 
        strip.background = element_blank(),
          legend.background = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank(), #transparent panel bg
          plot.background = element_blank(), #transparent plot bg
        aspect.ratio = 1
        )# + geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 1)

save_ggplot_formats(pathway_boxplot,
                    base_plot_dir = plots_folder,
                    plt_name = "boxplot_notch_gsva",
                    width = 2,
                    height = 2)
```


#Transcription factor analysis
```{r decoupler transcription factor analysis}

net_tf <- get_collectri(organism = 'mouse', split_complexes = TRUE)
net_tf

sample_acts_tf <- run_wmean(mat = cm.norm,
                            net = net_tf, 
                            .source='source', 
                            .target='target',
                            .mor='mor', 
                            times = 500, 
                            minsize = 5
                            )

n_tfs <- 100

# Transform to wide matrix
sample_acts_tf_mat <- sample_acts_tf %>%
  filter(statistic == 'norm_wmean') %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = 'condition', names_from = 'source',
              values_from = 'score') %>%
  column_to_rownames('condition') %>%
  as.matrix()

# Get top tfs with more variable means across clusters
tfs <- sample_acts_tf %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(std = sd(score)) %>%
  arrange(-abs(std)) %>%
  head(n_tfs) %>%
  pull(source)

sample_acts_tf_mat <- sample_acts_tf_mat[,tfs]

# Scale per sample
sample_acts_tf_mat <- scale(sample_acts_tf_mat)

# Choose color palette
palette_length = 100
my_color = colorRampPalette(c("darkblue", "white","red"))(palette_length)

my_breaks <- c(seq(-3, 0, length.out = ceiling(palette_length/2) + 1),
               seq(0.05, 3, length.out = floor(palette_length/2))
               )

# Plot
tf_heatmap <- pheatmap(t(sample_acts_tf_mat), border_color = NA,
                       cluster_rows = FALSE,cluster_cols = FALSE,color = my_color
                       )
```

```{r Save data}
png(file.path(plots_folder,"png",glue("progeny_transcription_factor_heatmap_{condition}.png")),
    width = 20,height = 40,units = "cm",res = 300)
draw(tf_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()

pdf(file.path(plots_folder,"pdf",glue("progeny_transcription_factor_heatmap_{condition}.pdf")),
    width = 20,height = 40)
draw(tf_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()
```
